one_time_description: Advanced costs received from Federal Government restricted for litigation costs, held in trust by the Nation's lawyer
economic_notes: Nation has investments in oilfield services (Seven Lakes Oilfield Services LP 8.33%), well servicing (Pimee Well Servicing LP 16.67% and Pimee Well Servicing Ltd. 16.67%), and metalworks (SLS Metalworks & Fabricating LP 8.33%). Also holds investments in BLCN Holdings and Amisk Ketuskeno Holdings as government business enterprises. Busy Beaver Community Store operated as consolidated entity. Fibre optics revenue of $641,708 received. Cenovus housing contributions received.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED due to unrecorded asset retirement obligations on tangible capital assets - auditors unable to determine the effect on expenses, surplus and net financial assets. Nation has a complex structure with 10 fully consolidated entities and 4 GBEs accounted for by modified equity method plus 4 business partnerships also on modified equity. $2.6M in advanced costs received from Federal Government for litigation (net $1.13M deferred). Total government transfers calculated as ISC $12,936,923 + ISC Health Canada $4,487,149 + CMHC $124,231 + HRDC $458,581 + First Nation Development Funds $853,829 (provincial) = $18,860,713 less FNDF which is provincial; ISC-only federal transfers = $17,188,132 plus CMHC and HRDC. OSR calculated as total revenue $30,917,667 minus total government transfers of $18,049,184. Remuneration schedule not included in provided text. Prior year accumulated surplus of $40,500,515 noted as opening balance for 2023, suggesting a large prior year adjustment or restatement.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
